channel.title: clustering on Luke Salamone's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in clustering on Luke Salamone's Blog

item.title: GMMs for Dummies (part 1)
item.description: MathJax.Hub.Config({ tex2jax: { inlineMath: [['$','$'], ['\\(','\\)']], displayMath: [['$$','$$'], ['\[','\]']], processEscapes: true, processEnvironments: true, skipTags: ['script', 'noscript', 'style', 'textarea', 'pre'], TeX: { equationNumbers: { autoNumber: "AMS" }, extensions: ["AMSmath.js", "AMSsymbols.js"] } } }); MathJax.Hub.Queue(function() { // Fix tags after MathJax finishes running. This is a // hack to overcome a shortcoming of Markdown. Discussion at // https://github.com/mojombo/jekyll/issues/199 var all = MathJax.Hub.getAllJax(), i; for(i = 0; i This article is an overview of the fit method of a Gaussian Mixture Model.

item.title: Gaussian Mixture Models for Dummies
item.description: Are you studying machine learning and want to know more about Gaussian Mixture Models? You’ve come to the right place. I have found other online resources to be difficult to approach and/or lacking crucial details. Here I will try to explain GMMs in plain language. Overview Gaussian Mixture Models are used to group data points into different clusters. In this way, it is similar to K-means clustering, where each cluster is specified by a point at its center.
